name: Sacedón - Córcoles – circular por La Isabela y embalse de Entrepeñas
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Sacedón are characterized by a diverse landscape, featuring the prominent Entrepeñas and Buendía reservoirs. The terrain includes olive groves, vineyards, and extensive pine forests, with routes traversing a mix of tracks and dirt roads. Riders can expect varied elevation changes across the region's valleys and hills. Best mountain bike trails around Sacedón The most popular mountain bike trail is Sacedón - Córcoles – circular por La Isabela y embalse de Entrepeñas, a 22.4 miles (36.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete, passing by the Entrepeñas reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sacedón - Dique de Pareja – circular por el embalse de Entrepeñas, a moderate 24.2 miles (39.0 km) path offering views of the Entrepeñas reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Ruinas romanas de Ercávica – circular desde Cañaveruelas por Alcohujate, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Mountain biking around Sacedón is defined by its large reservoirs, pine forests, and agricultural lands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sacedón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Sacedón?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sacedón offer a diverse landscape. You'll ride through olive groves, vineyards, and extensive pine forests. The routes are a mix of tracks and dirt roads, with some paths featuring clay and gypsum soil, which can be challenging during wet periods. The area also includes varied elevation changes across its valleys and hills, often providing stunning views of the Entrepeñas and Buendía reservoirs.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Sacedón, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around Sacedón listed in this guide. The routes cater to various skill levels, with 1 easy trail, 9 moderate trails, and 3 difficult trails, ensuring options for most riders.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Sacedón?
A: Yes, Sacedón offers options suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018000087/ruinas-romanas-de-ercavica-circular-desde-canaveruelas-por-alcohuja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruinas romanas de Ercávica – circular desde Cañaveruelas por Alcohujate&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 22 km circular trail that leads through rural landscapes, making it a good choice for a less strenuous family outing.

Q: What are the best times of year to go mountain biking in Sacedón?
A: Sacedón is generally enjoyable for mountain biking for much of the year. However, it's advisable to be cautious during wet periods, especially if riding on paths with clay and gypsum soil, as they can become slippery. Spring and autumn often provide pleasant temperatures and conditions, while summer mornings or late afternoons can be ideal to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Sacedón?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Sacedón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003315446/sacedon-corcoles-circular-por-la-isabela-y-embalse-de-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacedón - Córcoles – circular por La Isabela y embalse de Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt;, which passes by the Entrepeñas reservoir, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003555010/sacedon-dique-de-pareja-circular-por-el-embalse-de-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacedón - Dique de Pareja – circular por el embalse de Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the same reservoir.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The Sacedón area is rich in history. Along your rides, you might encounter the ruins of the ancient village of La Isabela Settlement, an old spa and psychiatric hospital now above water again. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, or visit religious sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3235056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4313970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there good viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning views, particularly over the Entrepeñas and Buendía reservoirs. You can find excellent vantage points such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Dam Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the Tagus Valley and the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What other outdoor activities can be combined with mountain biking in Sacedón?
A: Sacedón is a hub for various outdoor pursuits. The Entrepeñas and Buendía reservoirs are perfect for water sports like swimming, fishing, sailing, kayaking, wakeboarding, and windsurfing. The region also offers hiking trails and climbing opportunities, including the 'Boca del infierno' (Gates of Hell) via ferrata, making it a versatile destination for adventurers.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Sacedón?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead may vary, Sacedón is generally accessible, and being about an hour from Madrid and half an hour from Guadalajara, it's accustomed to visitors. You can typically find parking in or near the main towns and villages that serve as starting points for the trails, such as Sacedón itself or Cañaveruelas.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Sacedón?
A: Sacedón and its surrounding villages offer various amenities for visitors. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options to refuel and rest after your rides. The area is a popular tourist destination, especially around the reservoirs, so there are choices for different preferences and budgets.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Sacedón offers difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14885864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Entrepeñas Dam loop from Peñalagos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 45 km with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding ride with rewarding views.

Q: What are the main natural features that define the mountain biking experience in Sacedón?
A: The mountain biking experience in Sacedón is largely defined by its prominent natural features: the vast Entrepeñas and Buendía reservoirs, which provide stunning water views and a unique backdrop. Beyond the water, riders will traverse extensive pine forests, agricultural lands with olive groves and vineyards, and a mix of valleys and hills that create varied and engaging terrain.
tips: La Isabela was a spa town founded in 1826 on the banks of the Guadiela River, which was later submerged due to the construction of the Buendía reservoir in the early 1950s. Today some ruins are visible depending on the water level and you can go to they.

Just 5 minutes from the town of Córcoles are the ruins of the monastery that was part of the Cistercian complex dating from the second half of the 12th century and is a clear example of the presence of this Order throughout the Iberian Peninsula. The ruins can be visited, check the official website:
https://cultura.castillalamancha.es/patrimonio/yacimientos-visitables/monasterio-de-monsalud
Panoramic views of the Entrepeñas reservoir.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud
